methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
General Galactic is a Seed-stage climate & energy company founded by Halen Mattison and Luke Neise, having raised $10M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy) valued between $110M and $200M at Series A, supplemented by reference to a broader frontier-energy comp universe including 8 private firms and 1 public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $10M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $25M. Peer-median analysis benchmarks against the 3-company sample, which presents a median valuation of $150M with a $110M–$200M range. This method assumes General Galactic will command a comparable exit or interim valuation despite earlier stage positioning.

The two methods produce a wide but defensible range. The low-end estimate of $21M reflects the stage-progression method with modest haircut. The median estimate of $88M represents the arithmetic midpoint between methods. The high-end estimate of $172M approaches peer medians, appropriate if the company demonstrates traction approaching Series A thresholds. Key uncertainties include actual commercial progress relative to peers, capital efficiency, and sector-specific technical risk. The wide $21M–$172M range reflects appropriate caution given Seed stage and the heterogeneity of frontier-energy businesses.
